en: When can hair be countable Let's look at two sentences I've got grey hair
ko: hair는 언제 가산이 될까? 두 문장을 보자. 흰머리가 있어

en: I've got some grey hairs In this sentence the speaker's hair is completely grey That's
ko: 흰머리카락 몇 개가 있어. 첫 번째 문장에서 화자의 머리는 완전히 하얘. 왜냐면

en: because hair is uncountable when we talk about hair in general In the second sentence the speaker's
ko: 일반적으로 머리카락을 말할 때 hair는 불가산이거든. 두 번째 문장에서 화자의

en: hair is brown but he has two or three grey hairs That's because hair
ko: 머리는 갈색인데 흰머리카락이 두세 개 있어. 왜냐면 hair는

en: is countable when we talk about individual strands of hair We can also count locks
ko: 개별 머리카락을 말할 때 가산이거든. locks of

en: of hair which is a bunch of strands and a clump of hair which is
ko: hair(머리카락 한 타래)도 셀 수 있고, clump of hair(머리카락 뭉치)는

en: a large mass of hair There's a clump of hair in the shower Can you get rid of it please How
ko: 큰 덩어리를 말해. 샤워실에 머리카락 뭉치가 있어, 치워줄래?

en: would you complain to the waiter if your soup looked like this
ko: 수프가 이렇게 생겼으면 웨이터한테 뭐라고 불만을 말할래?
